_meta.description: Explicit lifecycle and state-contract index for canonical business objects.

stateModels.purpose: Control customer complaint, follow-up, escalation, and closure so service issues remain governed and traceable instead of disappearing into touchpoint notes.
Capture customer commitment as governed demand so commercial promise, SO linkage, and downstream delivery remain traceable from the first accepted PO.
notes: There is no separate status-options registry set yet; service logic is the current runtime truth.

stateModels.purpose: Control commercial estimation, review, offer release, and conversion so customer demand does not bypass governed quote truth before SO commitment.
notes: The live quote runtime uses internal_review/accepted/rejected/revised/converted. Legacy status-options aliases still expose review/won/lost and must not override QuoteService truth.

stateModels.purpose: Capture structured quote win-loss and effectiveness analysis so commercial learning, margin review, and continuous improvement are preserved as governed assessment records instead of ad hoc notes.
Turn accepted commercial demand into governed revenue-producing execution demand spanning engineering, production release, shipment gate, and financial realization.
notes: The runtime sales-order workflow uses quoted/cancelled, while the legacy status-options alias still exposes engineering_ready and omits cancelled. The authored package is the current runtime truth.

stateModels.purpose: Control AP three-way match, hold, posting, payment, and close so procurement correction cannot bypass financial truth.
notes: The operational status source is dispute_status. invoice_status may still exist as descriptive or compatibility data and must not override workflow truth.

stateModels.purpose: Controls invoice payment state, dispute handling, and financial close visibility before cash recognition is considered complete.
Represent customer receivable truth from shipment or billing release through dispute and settlement, without depending on a mixed AP/AR legacy object.
notes: AR still relies on the legacy ap_ar_invoices payment-state contract for workflow truth. A dedicated AR lifecycle surface should replace this bridge in a later phase.

stateModels.purpose: Control governed posting-date exceptions so backdated financial or logistics adjustments cannot bypass closed-period discipline.
notes: Creation immediately grants approved status when the exception passes validation and signature requirements.

stateModels.purpose: Represent governed downward financial adjustments so AP and AR corrections remain explicit and auditable instead of hiding inside a mixed invoice object.
notes: The current runtime exposes creation and retrieval only. Posting and settlement remain downstream ERP concerns, so approved is the authoritative state held here.

stateModels.purpose: Represent governed upward financial adjustments so AP and AR correction exposure stays explicit, traceable, and separate from mixed invoice truth.
notes: The current runtime exposes creation and retrieval only. Posting and settlement remain downstream ERP concerns, so approved is the authoritative state held here.

stateModels.purpose: Represent capitalization decisions that convert spend into governed asset value so maintenance, finance, and asset master stay aligned.
stateModels.workflowSource: fin_fixed_assets asset-status bridge pending dedicated capitalization runtime
notes: Dedicated capitalization runtime is not active yet. This package makes the business meaning explicit while workflow truth is still partially bridged from asset accounting surfaces.

stateModels.purpose: Control accounting period close and reopen decisions so posting gates remain explicit, auditable, and separable from invoice records.
notes: The service creates a close control directly in closed state and allows governed reopen/close transitions afterward.

stateModels.purpose: Control MES-ERP mismatch handling so interface drift is triaged, compensated, and closed instead of patched outside governed integration flow.
Control return authorization, receipt, evaluation, disposition, and closure so reverse-logistics truth stays linked to quality and finance outcomes.
Control cycle-count scheduling and closure so stock accuracy is corrected through governed counting instead of ad hoc inventory adjustment.
stateModels.workflowSource: wms_cycle_count_plans status plus warehouse governance bridge
notes: The underlying status column remains simple, so this contract provides the governed meaning of publish/pause/complete/close until a richer native state surface is introduced.

stateModels.purpose: Controls freight execution from booking through delivery closure so shipment release, carrier handoff, and proof-of-delivery stay under governed logistics truth.
Controls release, obsolescence, and inventory-usable truth for the shared item master without duplicating inventory item identity.
notes: Canonical inventory-items still converges from the legacy items runtime surface. The contract locks business meaning before API/domain renaming.

stateModels.purpose: Controls movement posting, hold, and reversal so inventory truth remains reconcilable under corrections and audit.
notes: Canonical inventory-movements still rides the inventory.inventory_transactions runtime surface while contract naming converges around inventory truth.

stateModels.purpose: Control outbound release, dispatch, delivery confirmation, and closure so OTIF and export-release truth stay governed from shipment creation to proof of delivery.
Controls warehouse release, hold, and archive so blocked storage areas cannot silently remain active for receiving, picking, or shipment.
Controls 5S audit planning, execution, follow-up, verification, and closure so visual-factory discipline becomes a governed lean loop instead of a score-only snapshot.
Controls engineering change request progression under guarded runtime so release governance exists before any separate PLM workflow engine is introduced.
Control incident reporting, containment, investigation, corrective action, and closure so safety events cannot escape the EHS governance loop.
Controls preventive-maintenance plan approval, release, hold, and retirement so recurring PM cadence stays auditable before work orders are auto-released.
Control preventive and corrective maintenance execution so equipment intervention, spare usage, and closeout stay governed instead of being hidden in note-only maintenance history.
Controls permit submission, approval, activation, suspension, expiry, and closure so hazardous or regulated work cannot proceed outside a governed permit-to-work register.
Controls safety good-catch and hazard observations through assignment, containment, and closure so frontline EHS risks cannot disappear into note-only reporting.
Provide a canonical asset identity so finance, maintenance, and customer-installed asset references do not split into separate truths.
notes: Canonical asset identity is still partially bridged from finance and service asset legacy surfaces until a dedicated unified asset runtime is completed.

stateModels.purpose: Controls fixed-asset operational availability, hold, retirement, and disposal governance for accounting and maintenance alignment.
Controls obligation due, waiver, and closure so permits, audits, and regulatory actions have a governed compliance anchor.
Controls customer commercial release eligibility, order acceptance, and evidence retention on the customer master.
Controls labor qualification, access eligibility, and employee release into production and compliance scopes.
Controls equipment qualification, lockout, and usable-machine release before maintenance, calibration, and production execution.
Provide a canonical measuring-device contract so calibration release, quarantine, and metrology evidence stop drifting between tool-control naming and legacy gauge terminology.
stateModels.workflowSource: wave1_lifecycle_normalization shared tools status plus calibration evidence bridge
notes: A dedicated measuring_devices table and runtime surface are not materialized yet.
Current lifecycle truth is bridged through shared tool-status control plus calibration and OOT evidence.

stateModels.purpose: Controls supplier qualification and spend authorization before purchasing or receiving release.
notes: Canonical suppliers still rides the purchasing.vendors runtime surface. The contract makes supplier-master meaning explicit without a data move.

stateModels.purpose: Control tool readiness, wear containment, and retirement before a tool can be issued to production, maintenance, or calibration use.
Controls tool and measuring-device release, wear containment, and retirement before use on production or inspection operations.
Capture machine-originated alarm and fault events with operator acknowledgement, escalation, downtime linkage, and maintenance follow-up without manually inventing machine data.
stateModels.workflowSource: workflow-library wf_mes_machine_alarm bridged through GenericCrud workflow-engine runtime

stateModels.purpose: Controls shopfloor dispatch release, hold, and execution closure so operations cannot start or disappear from queue outside the governed MES release horizon.
Control in-process quality checks so process escapes trigger hold, reinspection, or NCR before final inspection and shipment release.
Translate confirmed sales demand into governed production intent so materials, routing, and downstream WO dispatch stay synchronized with commercial commitment.
notes: Legacy status registry still exposes draft and omits on_hold/cancelled. The authored package follows the governed order runtime.

stateModels.purpose: Controls operation-level execution, hold, completion, and scrap so genealogy and in-process quality can anchor to the actual operation step.
notes: Production operations are contained-child workflow records. They must not be treated as an independent parent lifecycle authority.

stateModels.purpose: Control factory plan review, publication, freeze, and completion so dispatch, capacity, and OTIF commitments all read the same released schedule truth.
Control executable shopfloor release, execution, and quality hold at the operation level so production truth is governed where work is actually performed.
Gate inbound material before stock release so supplier quality, quarantine, and NCR escalation are recorded from a governed incoming inspection object.
Control governed correction and reversal after receipt, IQC, or invoice impact so partial completion can be reconciled through traceable recovery instead of manual patches.
Control physical receipt, quarantine, acceptance, putaway, and reversal so stock and AP matching cannot outrun receiving truth.
Authorize buy demand before sourcing or PO release so procurement cannot bypass planning, budget, or gate controls.
Control inbound advance shipment notice, discrepancy handling, and receiving readiness so physical receipts cannot outrun supplier intent and dock planning truth.
Authorize governed external spend and supplier commitment so receiving, IQC, and AP matching cannot bypass approved procurement intent.
notes: Canonical supplier-purchase-orders still rides the purchasing.purchase_orders runtime surface. The authored package records that compatibility alias explicitly.

stateModels.purpose: Control internal and supplier audit planning, execution, verification, and closure so compliance evidence and systemic findings remain governed from schedule through improvement follow-up.
Control temporary acceptance or shipment-release exception decisions so quality deviation from requirement cannot bypass governed approval and traceability.
notes: Expiry is tracked through effective dates and evidence, not a separate terminal workflow state in the current runtime.

stateModels.purpose: Govern APQP/FMEA-linked control plans so product and process characteristics, control methods, reaction plans, approvals, revisions, and shop-floor inspection execution stay traceable.
stateModels.workflowSource: workflow-library wf_fmea bridged through GenericCrud workflow-engine runtime

stateModels.purpose: Drive verified corrective and preventive action so recurring quality loss is closed through owned tasks, effectiveness review, and audit trace.
Control temporary process, product, or documentation deviations so exception handling remains explicit, time-bounded, and auditable instead of disappearing inside generic status notes.

Preserve the final-quality-control vocabulary used by operations while resolving it onto the governed outgoing-inspection truth so frontend and AI tooling stop treating FQC as a separate storage authority.
notes: FQC is retained as an operational alias only.
The live runtime and storage truth remain OQC, and no second FQC controller should be introduced.

stateModels.purpose: Control lean improvement intake, approval, implementation, verification, and closure so kaizen and systemic improvement work produces auditable benefit instead of unmanaged ideas.
Capture measurement-system-analysis and GRR evidence so acceptance of measurement capability is explicit before relying on data for release, SPC, or capability decisions.
Contain and disposition quality escapes from incoming, in-process, outgoing, or returned product so no defect signal disappears outside a governed NCR record.
Control outgoing quality release so finished goods cannot pass to shipment without governed final inspection truth.
notes: Legacy LogisticsController paths still use pass/fail/conditional values in some compatibility flows. The governed runtime truth for the canonical object is pending/accepted/rejected/waived.

stateModels.purpose: Controls final and outbound quality disposition using the existing result column until a richer outbound inspection split is required.
Govern temporary override, waiver, and release bypass decisions so exceptional shipments or process gates remain explicit, timeboxed, signed, and reviewable.
Preserve structured statistical-process-control observations so process capability, rule violations, and escalation triggers are governed assessment evidence instead of spreadsheet-only history.
Provide forward and backward genealogy for lot, serial, batch, and production-order relationships so containment, recall, NCR, and customer-return decisions can be traced to source records.
stateModels.workflowSource: PostgreSQL traceability tables exposed through GenericCrud runtime
